Values are current to 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you up-to-date.

explanation:
**RAID capacity calculators** help you plan storage infrastructure: drives, redundancy, uptime SLA, cloud costs. The right decision scales your business.

## How This Works

This calculator computes usable capacity and fault tolerance based on your RAID level and drive configuration. Enter the number of disks and capacity per disk to see how much storage is available after accounting for redundancy overhead.

## Key RAID Levels

Each RAID configuration trades off capacity for redundancy:
- **RAID 0**: Maximum capacity, zero fault tolerance (striping only)
- **RAID 1**: 50% usable capacity, mirrors data across drives
- **RAID 5**: (N-1) usable capacity, tolerates one drive failure
- **RAID 6**: (N-2) usable capacity, tolerates two drive failures
- **RAID 10**: 50% usable capacity, combines mirroring and striping

## Final Notes

This calculator is a reference tool. For critical infrastructure decisions, consult with an IT professional. Values are current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: RAID 0 (striping) maximizes capacity by distributing data across all drives with no redundancy. One drive failure loses everything. Use only for non-critical data like caches.
RAID 1 (mirroring) duplicates data across two drives, giving 50% usable capacity but perfect redundancy. If one drive fails, the other has an identical copy.
RAID 5 distributes data and parity across N drives. You get (N-1) usable capacity and can survive one drive failure. Common for balanced storage with redundancy.
RAID 6 extends RAID 5 with dual parity across N drives. You get (N-2) usable capacity and can survive up to two simultaneous drive failures.
RAID 10 (1+0) mirrors your data and then stripes across mirrors. You get 50% usable capacity with excellent performance and can tolerate multiple drive failures.
Yes, all our calculators are free and require no account or registration. No ads, no paywalls.
These use industry-standard RAID formulas and are reference calculations. For critical enterprise deployments, consult with storage professionals.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We never send your inputs to any server or store anything.

faq.q: How do I choose a RAID level?
faq.a: Choose based on your priority: RAID 0 for speed and capacity with no redundancy, RAID 1 for simplicity, RAID 5 for balanced performance and protection, RAID 6 for high reliability, RAID 10 for performance and protection combined.
